exemplar.title:
âIs subjectivity overly celebrated in the arts but unfairly condemned in history?
Discuss with reference to the arts and history.â

detailedExemplarData.title:
âIs subjectivity overly celebrated in the arts but unfairly condemned in history?
Discuss with reference to the arts and history.â

strengths: Sustained focus on the prescribed title with precise definitions of key terms (âoverly celebrated,â âunfairly condemned,â âsubjectivityâ)
Clear, coherent argumentation linked to both the arts and history areas of knowledge
Effective use of discipline-specific real-world examples (e.g., AbramoviÄâs Rhythm 0, Lacyâs The Crystal Quilt, Dalit literature, the comfort-women debate, The Lost Cause)
Awareness of and engagement with contrasting perspectives and ethical implications
Conclusion demonstrates reflection on broader significance and need for ethical frameworks
weaknesses: Analysis occasionally slips into descriptive narrative rather than sustained evaluation of how examples advance the central claim
Evaluation of different points of view is sometimes asserted rather than critically compared in depth
Implications of arguments are noted but not systematically explored or synthesized across the essay
Limited explicit use of TOK terminology (e.g., personal vs shared knowledge, bias, ways of knowing) to deepen theoretical links
Conclusion largely summarizes earlier points instead of fully integrating insights from both AOKs
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The official examinerâs total of 9 reflects recognition that this essay operates at the top of the 7-8 band and approaches 9-10-level performance. While some analysis remains descriptive and evaluation of alternative perspectives is not always fully developed, the response maintains a sustained, coherent focus on the title, uses highly relevant and discipline-specific examples, and shows clear awareness of ethical implications and wider significance. These strengths justify elevating the essay into the 9-10 band. Minor weaknessesâmainly in depth of evaluation and explicit TOK terminologyâdo not significantly detract from an overall performance that merits 9 out of 10.

annotations.comment: The Sophie Calle âVenetian Suiteâ discussion is descriptive; it would benefit from deeper evaluation of how her subjective approach challenges ethical norms and reflects on the nature of subjectivity in art.
The ethical implications are noted but could be enriched by explicit TOK terminology (e.g., bias, perspective, evidence) to tie the analysis back to theory of knowledge concepts.
The comfort women case is compelling but lacks engagement with Ramseyerâs methodology and source selection. Critically assessing these aspects would expose potential biases in his argument.

The AbramoviÄ âRhythm 0â example is descriptive but the analysis of its significance to subjectivityâs ethical implications is cursory. The student should evaluate how this case advances the central claim rather than merely recount events.
Analysis of the Lacy example remains largely descriptive. The student should critically examine how ethical frameworks were upheld and why this distinguishes it from ethically problematic art.

The introduction effectively defines the key terms of the title, establishing a clear foundation for discussing âoverly celebrated,â âunfairly condemnedâ and âsubjectivityâ in the arts and history, which supports a focused exploration of the prescribed title.
The thesis remains implicit rather than framed as a clear knowledge question. The student should restate the prescribed title as an explicit TOK question to sharpen the essayâs investigative focus.
The connection between the Marcus Aurelius quote and the core knowledge question is underdeveloped. The student should analyse how this philosophical perspective frames subjectivity in both AOKs.
The paragraph ends abruptly with an incomplete sentence. The student should ensure full sentence structure and use clear transitions to maintain coherence.

The assertion that subjectivity âpushes boundariesâ in postmodern art is broad and lacks support. The student should provide concrete examples or theoretical justification to substantiate this claim.

The essay uses multiple arts examples but lacks comparative analysis between them. Contrasting Lacyâs ethical practice with Calleâs risky approach would strengthen the argument about subjectivityâs limits.
The Dalit literature example effectively demonstrates how subjectivity can reveal marginalized historical perspectives, linking well to the history AOK and enriching the discussion.

The Suzanne Lacy âThe Crystal Quiltâ example is well chosen to illustrate how subjectivity can empower marginalised voices while respecting ethical boundaries, demonstrating effective linkage to the arts AOK.
The inclusion of âThe Lost Causeâ effectively illustrates the risks of historical revisionism and grounds the discussion in a well-known example of subjectivityâs misuse in history.
The analysis of the âLost Causeâ remains descriptive. The student should compare it with mainstream historiography to critically assess why its subjective narrative was rightfully condemned.
The conclusionâs emphasis on implementing ethical frameworks and acknowledging bias demonstrates awareness of the broader implications of subjectivity for responsible knowledge production.
The conclusion largely reiterates earlier points. To achieve greater depth, the student should synthesise the implications across both AOKs rather than summarise.
The opening claim about historyâs emphasis on objective evidence would benefit from explicit reference to TOK frameworks such as ways of knowing and the knowledge framework to deepen the analysis.

The discussion of Dalit criticsâ early labels is asserted without examining their motivations. Introducing a counterclaim from critics would provide a more balanced evaluation of subjectivity in history.
The discussion on inevitable subjectivity could be strengthened by integrating TOK distinctions between personal and shared knowledge to clarify why subjectivity persists in historical research.
The concluding statement restates the thesis without synthesizing earlier implications. The student should integrate insights from both AOKs to provide a coherent resolution.

The section on nationalist versus Marxist historians shows awareness of differing methodologies but lacks critical comparison. The student should evaluate how each approach shapes subjectivity in historical interpretation.
The final paragraph would benefit from an explicit restatement of the prescribed title to reinforce cohesion and remind the reader of the central TOK question.
The call for âresponsible subjectivityâ is prescriptive. Reframing these recommendations as critical reflections on the knowledge question will maintain an evaluative TOK tone.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
